response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced polyglot engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries) and direct IDE development experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI). However, the role's core requirements — strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience — are essentially absent. Java appears only peripherally, Kotlin and Spring not at all, and recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms rather than JVM enterprise tooling.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI, C/C++)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling (D test runner, fluent-asserts, MCP code generators)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, testing workshops and courses)
concerns: No Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience listed
Java exposure is minimal and dated; no evidence of deep JVM expertise
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), not enterprise JVM tooling
